# Third-party action pin manifest — all workflows
#
# Every action used by the repository workflows is pinned to a full commit
# SHA. This file records the human-readable tag for each pinned SHA so
# reviewers can verify pins without leaving the repo.
#
# To bump a pin:
#   1. Look up the desired release on GitHub.
#   2. Resolve the tag → commit SHA via
#      `gh api /repos/<owner>/<repo>/git/ref/tags/<tag> --jq .object.sha`
#   3. Update both the workflow YAML AND this file.
#   4. Open a PR labeled `deps`.
#
actions/checkout
  pinned_sha:     9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
  pinned_tag:     v7.0.0
  used_in:        ci.yml
                  consumer-smoke.yml
                  release.yml
                  upstream-drift.yml

actions/setup-dotnet
  pinned_sha:     a98b56852c35b8e3190ac28c8c2271da59106c68
  pinned_tag:     v6.0.0
  used_in:        ci.yml
                  consumer-smoke.yml
                  release.yml

actions/upload-artifact
  pinned_sha:     043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a
  pinned_tag:     v7.0.1
  used_in:        ci.yml

irongut/CodeCoverageSummary
  pinned_sha:     51cc3a756ddcd398d447c044c02cb6aa83fdae95
  pinned_tag:     v1.3.0
  used_in:        ci.yml

release-drafter/release-drafter
  pinned_sha:     da8b9b696abad2ad997580e49f4c1c2e03cc2bd2
  pinned_tag:     v7.3.0
  used_in:        release-drafter-draft.yml (root action)
                  release-drafter-autolabel.yml (autolabeler subpath)
  source_citation: |
    Exact line proving `pull_request: edited` is handled by the
    autolabeler (per PLAN-RELEASE-WORKFLOW.md acceptance):
      src/actions/autolabeler/main.ts:70
      "if (matcher.test(payload.pull_request.title)) {"
      https://github.com/release-drafter/release-drafter/blob/da8b9b696abad2ad997580e49f4c1c2e03cc2bd2/src/actions/autolabeler/main.ts#L70
    Above that, the early-exit at main.ts:17-22 only filters
    `context.eventName !== 'pull_request' && !== 'pull_request_target'`.
    It does NOT filter on `context.payload.action`, so any
    `pull_request` event type (opened, reopened, synchronize, edited,
    ...) reaches the title matcher. For `edited` events, GitHub sends
    the post-edit title in the payload (see
    https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks/webhook-events-and-payloads#pull_request),
    so the matcher operates on the new title.
